title_en: Belief in Angels as a Pillar of Faith
content_en:
Belief in the angels (al-iman bil-mala'ikah) is the second of the six pillars of Islamic faith (arkan al-iman), following belief in Allah and preceding belief in the divine books, the messengers, the Last Day, and the divine decree. Its inclusion as a pillar — not a secondary or optional doctrine — reflects the centrality of angelic reality to the Islamic understanding of how Allah relates to creation, how revelation was transmitted, and how the universe is administered.

The Quran explicitly links belief in angels to the definition of comprehensive faith: 'The Messenger has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and so have the believers. All of them have believed in Allah and His angels and His books and His messengers' (Al-Baqarah: 285). Denying the existence of angels, or denying specific doctrines about them that are established by clear textual proof, represents a deficiency in or denial of this pillar of faith.

What does belief in angels practically require? Al-Ashqar identifies several dimensions: affirming their existence as real, created beings (not symbolic or metaphorical figures); affirming their specific characteristics as described in revelation (created from light, possessing wings, sinless by nature); affirming their specific roles as described (carrying the Throne, delivering revelation, recording deeds, managing natural phenomena, being present at death and in the grave); and affirming what is known of specific named angels without elaborating beyond what revelation has established.

Belief in angels also has a profound devotional implication: it reminds the believer that they are never alone. Every act of worship is performed in the presence of noble witnesses. Every moment of struggle is known to beings of light who pray for the believer's forgiveness. Every circle of knowledge is attended by divine visitors. This awareness, consistently maintained, transforms the quality of worship from a private mechanical act to a participation in the cosmic worship that permeates the universe.

Al-Ashqar concludes his treatment of this pillar by noting the practical fruits of correct belief in angels: an increased sense of reverence and modesty before Allah's observers, a greater motivation for righteous speech and action, a deeper appreciation of the magnitude of divine revelation (which was carried to earth by the greatest of angels), and a more vivid expectation of the angelic presence at death — either as the ambassadors of mercy or as the enforcers of the decree. Belief in angels is thus not a metaphysical abstract but a living doctrine that, when genuinely held, shapes the believer's daily consciousness and long-term spiritual orientation.
title_id: Iman kepada Malaikat sebagai Rukun Iman
content_id:
Iman kepada malaikat (al-iman bil-malaika) adalah rukun kedua dari enam rukun iman Islam (arkan al-iman), setelah iman kepada Allah dan sebelum iman kepada kitab-kitab ilahi, para rasul, Hari Akhir, dan takdir ilahi. Pencantuman-nya sebagai rukun, bukan sebagai doktrin sekunder atau opsional, mencerminkan sentralitas realitas malaikat bagi pemahaman Islam tentang bagaimana Allah berhubungan dengan ciptaan, bagaimana wahyu disampaikan, dan bagaimana alam semesta dikelola.

Al-Quran secara eksplisit mengaitkan iman kepada malaikat dengan definisi iman yang komprehensif: Rasul telah beriman kepada apa yang diturunkan kepadanya dari Tuhannya, dan begitu pula orang-orang beriman. Semuanya beriman kepada Allah, malaikat-malaikat-Nya, kitab-kitab-Nya, dan rasul-rasul-Nya (Al-Baqarah: 285). Mengingkari keberadaan malaikat, atau mengingkari doktrin-doktrin spesifik tentang mereka yang ditetapkan oleh bukti tekstual yang jelas, merupakan kekurangan atau pengingkaran terhadap rukun iman ini.

Apa yang secara praktis diperlukan oleh iman kepada malaikat? Al-Asyqar mengidentifikasi beberapa dimensi: mengakui keberadaan mereka sebagai makhluk nyata yang diciptakan, bukan tokoh-tokoh simbolis atau metaforis; mengakui karakteristik spesifik mereka sebagaimana dijelaskan dalam wahyu, yaitu diciptakan dari cahaya, memiliki sayap, suci secara alami; mengakui peran-peran spesifik mereka sebagaimana dijelaskan, yaitu membawa Arsy, menyampaikan wahyu, mencatat amal perbuatan, mengelola fenomena alam, hadir pada saat kematian dan di dalam kubur; serta mengakui apa yang diketahui tentang malaikat-malaikat yang disebutkan namanya tanpa mengelaborasi melampaui apa yang ditetapkan wahyu.

Iman kepada malaikat juga memiliki implikasi ibadah yang mendalam: hal itu mengingatkan orang beriman bahwa mereka tidak pernah sendirian. Setiap amal ibadah dilakukan di hadapan saksi-saksi yang mulia. Setiap momen perjuangan diketahui oleh makhluk-makhluk cahaya yang mendoakan ampunan bagi orang beriman. Setiap majelis ilmu dihadiri oleh para tamu ilahi. Kesadaran ini, yang dijaga secara konsisten, mengubah kualitas ibadah dari tindakan mekanis yang pribadi menjadi partisipasi dalam ibadah kosmik yang meresap di seluruh alam semesta.

Al-Asyqar mengakhiri pembahasannya tentang rukun ini dengan mencatat buah-buah praktis dari keimanan yang benar kepada malaikat: meningkatnya rasa hormat dan kesopanan di hadapan para pengawas Allah, motivasi yang lebih besar untuk berucap dan berbuat dengan benar, penghargaan yang lebih dalam terhadap besarnya wahyu ilahi yang dibawa ke bumi oleh malaikat yang paling agung, dan harapan yang lebih jelas akan kehadiran malaikat pada saat kematian, baik sebagai duta rahmat maupun sebagai penegak keputusan. Iman kepada malaikat dengan demikian bukan merupakan abstraksi metafisik melainkan doktrin yang hidup yang, ketika diyakini dengan sungguh-sungguh, membentuk kesadaran sehari-hari dan orientasi spiritual jangka panjang seorang mukmin.
content_ar: الاعتقاد in the الملائكة (al-الإيمان bil-mala'ikah) is the ثانياً of the six pillars of Islamic الإيمان (arkan al-الإيمان), following الاعتقاد in الله and preceding الاعتقاد in the divine الكتب, the الرسل, the Last Day, and the divine decree. Its inclusion as a pillar — not a secondary or optional doctrine — reflects the centrality of angelic reality to the Islamic الفهم of how الله relates to creation, how الوحي was transmitted, and how the universe is administered. The القرآن الكريم explicitly links الاعتقاد in الملائكة to the definition of comprehensive الإيمان: 'الرسول ﷺ has believed in what was revealed to him from his Lord, and لذا have the المؤمنون. All of them have believed in الله and His الملائكة and His الكتب and His الرسل' (Al-Baqarah: 285). Denying the existence of الملائكة, or denying specific doctrines about them that are established by clear textual proof, represents a deficiency in or denial of this pillar of الإيمان. What does الاعتقاد in الملائكة practically require? Al-Ashqar identifies several dimensions: affirming their existence as real, created beings (not symbolic or metaphorical figures); affirming their specific characteristics as described in الوحي (created from light, possessing wings, sinless by nature); affirming their specific roles as described (carrying the Throne, delivering الوحي, recording deeds, managing natural phenomena, being present at الموت and in the القبر); and affirming what is known of specific named الملائكة without elaborating beyond what الوحي has established. الاعتقاد in الملائكة أيضاً has a profound devotional implication: it reminds the المؤمن that they are never alone. Every act of worship is performed in the presence of noble witnesses. Every moment of struggle is known to beings of light who pray for the المؤمن's المغفرة. Every circle of العلم is attended by divine visitors. This awareness, consistently maintained, transforms the quality of worship from a private mechanical act to a participation in the cosmic worship that permeates the universe. Al-Ashqar concludes his treatment of this pillar by noting the practical fruits of correct الاعتقاد in الملائكة: an increased sense of reverence and الحياء قبل الله's observers, a greater motivation for الصالح speech and action, a deeper appreciation of the magnitude of divine الوحي (which was carried to earth by the greatest of الملائكة), and a more vivid expectation of the angelic presence at الموت — either as the ambassadors of الرحمة or as the enforcers of the decree. الاعتقاد in الملائكة is وهكذا not a metaphysical abstract but a living doctrine that, عندما genuinely held, shapes the المؤمن's daily consciousness and long-term spiritual orientation.
